question: What is SuPrathon 2025?
answer: SuPrathon 2025 was India's largest national-level virtual hackathon, a 36-hour coding competition organized by SuPrazo Technologies. It targeted students, coders, and innovators to build projects in domains like Web Development, App Development, AI/ML, and Open Innovation.

question: Who organized SuPrathon 2025?
answer: SuPrathon 2025 was organized by SuPrazo Technologies, a company specializing in AI/ML automation and IT solutions. The company organizes SuPrathon as India's premier hackathon community to connect innovators nationwide.

question: When was SuPrathon 2025 held?
answer: The hackathon ran from July 18, 2025 (12:00 AM IST) to July 20, 2025 (12:00 PM IST). Registration closed on July 16, 2025, and results were announced on July 23, 2025.

question: How can I register for SuPrathon 2025?
answer: You can register by visiting https://suprathon.suprazotech.in  filling out the official Google Form, completing the UPI payment, and waiting for email confirmation with the join link. Only verified payments grant eligibility.

question: Is registration free?
answer: No, paid registration via UPI was required. Only verified payments were eligible.

question: What is the team size limit?
answer: Teams could have 1-4 members. Solo participation was allowed.

question: Who can participate in SuPrathon 2025?
answer: It was open to students, technologists, freelancers, and professionals from any background. No specific restrictions by location or age; primarily national-level but open to anyone who registered officially.

question: What domains were allowed in SuPrathon 2025?
answer: Participants could build projects in Web Development, App Development, AI/ML, and Open Innovation, solving real-world problems.

question: What were the submission requirements?
answer: Participants had to submit project title/domain, GitHub or Google Drive code/demo link, YouTube or Drive pitch video, PDF/Slides presentation deck, and a project description via the official Google Form. Late or non-form submissions were disqualified.

question: What were the judging criteria?
answer: Projects were evaluated based on Innovation & Creativity (originality), Technical Complexity (implementation depth), Impact & Usefulness (real-world relevance), and Execution & Completion (working prototype).

question: Who were the judges?
answer: The judging panel was led by Sumit Waghmare, CEO of CodeElevate Academy.

question: What are the prizes for SuPrathon 2025?
answer: Prizes: 1st Prize: Cash prizes, trophy, internships, and certificate; 2nd Prize: Goodies, internships, courses, and certificate; 3rd Prize: Courses and certificates; Most Innovative Project Award: Surprise gifts. All participants received national-level certificates and badges.

question: Will I get a certificate for participating?
answer: Yes, all participants who submitted their projects received national-level certificates and badges.

question: Will I get an internship?
answer: Internships were offered to top-performing teams and were not guaranteed for all participants.

question: Is plagiarism allowed?
answer: No. Plagiarism was strictly prohibited and led to immediate disqualification.

question: Who can I contact for doubts or technical issues?
answer: Participants could contact organizers via https://suprathon.suprazotech.in, phone 9665658240, or designated mentors for guidance. Technical issues during the event were handled via mentorship channels.

question: Was SuPrathon 2025 fully online?
answer: Yes, it was fully virtual with mentorship provided throughout via the join links sent after registration.

question: Who are you?
answer: I'm a SuPrathon chatbot designed to answer all your questions about SuPrathon 2025 and 2.0.
